what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment posted to the design-assets channel, pointing to an image resource at image://design-assets/icons/Vi.
observations: The file contains only YAML front matter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added timestamp.
The attachment type is 'images' and the target uses a custom image:// URI scheme.
The target path suggests it is an icon asset named 'Vi' under design-assets/icons.
No body content or symbols are present beyond the front matter.
standouts: Attachment record links a channel message to a specific design asset file.
concerns: The image:// URI scheme is not a standard file or HTTP reference; resolving it requires external context.
No checksum, size, or MIME type is recorded, so integrity or format cannot be verified from this file alone.
notes: This is a lightweight ledger entry that maps a design-assets channel attachment to an icon image resource via a custom URI.
questions: What protocol or resolver handles the image:// scheme?
Is 'Vi' a file name, an icon set, or a version identifier?
Are there other metadata fields such as author, dimensions, or format stored elsewhere?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose (attachment metadata) matches the content: it records an image attachment reference with a timestamp.
intention_signals: It appears intended to catalog image attachments in a channel so design assets can be traced back to their source messages and storage locations.
novelty: The use of a custom image:// URI for target rather than a relative file path or URL.
contribution: Provides a minimal, timestamped attachment record that connects a channel entry to a specific image asset location.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML front-matter attachment ledger entry for the design-assets channel that references an image asset at image://design-assets/icons/Vi with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To record and track an image attachment posted in the design-assets channel so the referenced icon asset remains discoverable.
